title: Try It Yourself - Cloning a GitHub Repository

content.problemStatement:
You want to bring an existing GitHub project to your local machine so you can start working on it. The project already exists on GitHub as a public repository. \
**Your job is to clone the repo to your computer**.

---

Steps to follow to clone the Repo are :
1. **Copy the repository URL** \
Go to the repo (https://github.com/codechef8899/clone-practice-repo) \
→ click Code → copy the HTTPS URL.

2. **Go to your terminal and run:** 
```sss
git clone 
```
You'll notice a folder created in your workspace named `clone-practice-repo`. \
You've just cloned a Github Repository!

---

**To Verify the clone** : \
Run:
```sss
cd clone-practice-repo
git remote -v
```
**Expected output:**

This confirms the repo is fully connected.

---
